title: Array Product Except Self
description: Given a non-empty array of integers nums with at least two elements, return a new array output where each element of the output[i] (at index i) is equal to the product of all numbers in nums except nums[i] (i.e., element at index i). For instance: Input=[1,2,3,4], Output=[24,12,8,6]
inputFormat: A non-empty array of integers nums with at least two elements
outputFormat: An integer array where output[i] is the product of all elements in nums except nums[i]
sampleSolution:
def solve(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    output = [1] * n

    prefix = 1
    for i in range(n):
        output[i] = prefix
        prefix *= nums[i]

    suffix = 1
    for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
        output[i] *= suffix
        suffix *= nums[i]

    return output
